title: Cannot Start Pod: infra-image throws invalid switch
body:
/kind bug
**Description**
Starting a pod after creation fails using locally generated podman-pause 4.0.x infrastructure image.  Logs indicate that catatonit is executing with an invalid -P switch

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Remove existing localhost/podman-pause and k8s.gcr.io/pause images
2. Create New Pod
3. Start New Pod
4. Examine `podman pod ls`  and `podman ps -a`.  Infrastructure Container will have Exited
5. Examine logs from infrastructure container 

**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ pod create test
Error: `podman pod create` takes no arguments
$ podman pod create --name test
1e611f9693d6abfc381dac717d2919a0ea7a4085bc9efb461042273864aa278b
$ podman pod start test
1e611f9693d6abfc381dac717d2919a0ea7a4085bc9efb461042273864aa278b
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS                    PORTS       NAMES
c166197c2f29  localhost/podman-pause:4.0.3-1649289600              10 seconds ago  Exited (1) 2 seconds ago              1e611f9693d6-infra
$ podman pod ls
POD ID        NAME        STATUS      CREATED         INFRA ID      # OF CONTAINERS
1e611f9693d6  test        Exited      38 seconds ago  c166197c2f29  1
$ podman logs 1e611f9693d6-infra
/catatonit: invalid option -- 'P'
usage: catatonit [-ghLV] [--] <progname> [<arguments>...]
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Pod Infrastructure Container should run and stay up

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Creating Pod with k8s.gcr.io/pause infrastructure image works without issues 
```
$ podman pod create --name test --infra-image k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.7
66a753026702425782e866c63ac7fc6843d8538b89b1f2f854775c8fe3d67e8b
$ podman pod start test
66a753026702425782e866c63ac7fc6843d8538b89b1f2f854775c8fe3d67e8b
$ podman pod ls 
POD ID        NAME        STATUS      CREATED         INFRA ID      # OF CONTAINERS
66a753026702  test        Running     35 seconds ago  42c27c895a71  1
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                 COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES
42c27c895a71  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.7              40 seconds ago  Up 10 seconds ago              66a753026702-infra
```
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Wed Apr  6 17:00:00 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.0.3-1.1.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: You need to update catatonit to version 0.1.7. Podman 4.0 depends on that.
